title: Configure Gemini Client for Computer Use
description: Set up the GenAI client with the ComputerUse tool enabled.

title: Fix TUI Thinking Overwrite & Final Response Display
description: The TUI currently only shows the latest 'thinking' event, which can cause important final responses to be overwritten or truncated in the header. We should move thinking events into the scrollable viewport or maintain a history of them.

title: Docs Interaction Diagram
description: Generate a Graphviz interaction diagram for the system architecture.

title: Secure Avatar & Auth
description: Implement secure session persistence and secret injection to allow authenticated agent workflows.

title: Implement Cookie Injection
description: Add a flag --cookies-file to main.go that loads JSON cookies and injects them into the chromedp context.

title: Emit Model Thoughts
description: Update computer.go to emit 'thinking' events when the model outputs text blocks, ensuring reasoning is visible.

title: Agent Observability & Debugging
description: Advanced debugging tools including 'Second Pair of Eyes' analysis and automated verification of agent actions.

title: Experiment: Second Pair of Eyes
description: Create a script that uses a lightweight model (Gemini Flash) to audit the 'post' screenshots and verify if the intended action had a visual effect.

title: Integrate Test Server into Dev Workflow
description: The cmd/testserver should be officially supported as a way to verify agent skills (scrolling, waiting, etc.) without external dependencies.

title: Verbose Execution Logging
description: Log the exact size of screenshot payloads and the duration of each sub-step (navigation, screenshotting, model inference) to identify bottlenecks.

title: Implement Debug Screenshot Dump
description: Save raw bytes of every attempted screenshot, even on failure, to a 'debug' folder with detailed browser state (URL, ReadyState).

title: Capture Console Logs
description: Stream browser console.log and errors into the session log to identify JS crashes on the target page.

title: Detailed Turn Reporting
description: Produce a JSON 'turn-log' after every cycle containing the exact ToolCall, ToolResponse, and timing.

title: Debug Screenshot Hang & Improve Interaction Reliability
description: Root cause and fix the intermittent hang during initial screenshotting and ensure Turn 1 proceeds consistently. This is the blocker for the Lights Experiment.

title: Add --show-browser flag
description: Add a CLI flag (e.g., --show-browser) to disable headless mode. This requires plumbing the boolean down to pkg/computer.Run and conditionally omitting chromedp.Headless from the allocator options.

title: Session Viewer: Backend API & Static Hosting
description: Implement a chi-based Go server that: 1. Scans 'logs/*.log' and 'screenshots/' to build a session manifest. 2. Provides /api/v1/sessions and /api/v1/sessions/{id}. 3. Serves the 'screenshots/' directory as a static file server. 4. Embeds/Serves the Lit frontend dist/ folder.

title: Rename project to riptide
description: Update go.mod, imports, and documentation to reflect the new project name 'riptide'.

title: Handle safety_decision
description: Handle the safety_decision argument from the model by acknowledging it in the tool response.

title: Enhance TUI with Emoji
description: Update pkg/tui/tui.go to use relevant emoji in the TUI header and log stream to improve visual distinction of events (e.g., 🧠 for thinking, 🛠️ for tools, ✅ for success, ❌ for errors).

title: Session Viewer: Integrated Build & Watch Mode
description: 1. Add a WebSocket hub to the backend for real-time turn updates. 2. Create a Makefile or task to run Vite build and move dist/ to backend for static embedding.

title: Agent Benchmarking & Resiliency
description: Establish a statistical framework to measure agent success rates, token efficiency, and robustness against complex UI scenarios.

title: Implement Capability Benchmarking Runner
description: Create a tool to run full agent loops against testserver scenarios repeatedly to measure Success Rate, Avg Turns, and Token Usage.

title: Research: Define Deterministic Success Criteria for Benchmarks
description: Define how we programmatically verify success for complex scenarios (e.g., 'did the specific data reach the database' vs 'did the model say it succeeded').

title: Promote MaxRecentScreenshots to Flag
description: Update main.go and computer.go to make the screenshot history limit configurable via a -max-screenshots flag (default 3).

title: Fix Chromedp Unmarshal Errors
description: Investigate and resolve 'cannot unmarshal JSON string into Go network.CookiePartitionKey' and 'unknown IPAddressSpace value' errors. These are likely caused by mismatches between the browser version and cdproto.

title: Interactive Safety Handling
description: Implement the 'Human-in-the-Loop' safety protocol. When the model signals a safety barrier (CAPTCHA), pause execution and request explicit user confirmation or assistance via the CLI/TUI.

title: Safety Decision Handler
description: Implement the logic to detect 'safety_decision' arguments in tool calls. Pause the loop, print the explanation to the user, and wait for a 'continue/terminate' signal (CLI input).

title: Interactive TUI with Bubble Tea
description: Replace standard logging with a rich Terminal UI using Bubble Tea, enabling real-time status monitoring and interactive user intervention.

title: Refactor Computer Loop for Observability
description: Update pkg/computer.Run to accept an event observer interface (for logs/status) and a safety handler callback.

title: Implement Bubble Tea Model
description: Create the TUI model with viewport for logs, spinner for status, and input handling for safety prompts.

title: Integrate TUI with Safety Decisions
description: Connect the safety handler callback to the TUI to pause execution and prompt the user for Yes/No confirmation.

title: Session Viewer: Research Tasker Architecture
description: Analyze the /home/hussain/projects/tasker repository to understand its Go RPC backend and Lit frontend structure. Identify patterns for API design, project layout, and WebComponent organization that can be leveraged for the Session Viewer.

title: Session Management
description: Implement Session UUIDs to organize screenshots and artifacts per run.

title: Log Initial Prompt
description: Update computer.Run to emit the initial user prompt as a structured event for better audit trails.

title: Update Documentation
description: Update README, GEMINI.md, and .gitignore with new conventions and usage.

title: Epic: Session Viewer (Lit + Go)
description: Build a web-based viewer for agent sessions. Features: 1. Go backend to serve logs/screenshots and provide a JSON API. 2. Lit WebComponents frontend to browse session history and view turn-by-turn galleries.

title: Advanced Tool Mapping & Prompt Fallbacks
description: Explore ways to handle tool hallucinations more robustly: 1. Maintain a registry of built-in mappings (e.g., 'search' -> 'navigate'). 2. Allow users to define their own mappings via config. 3. Implement a 'Reflection' fallback where Gemini is used to rewrite the model's failed intent into a valid tool call.

title: Refactor & Hardening: Transition to Modular Platform
description: Focus on architectural maintenance, utility functionality, and streamlined onboarding to move Riptide from prototype to a scalable framework.

title: Implement Tool Registry Pattern in Executor
description: Refactor pkg/computer/executor.go to move away from the massive switch statement. Implement a registry for tools/skills to allow for easier extension without modifying core logic.

title: Unify Build & Test Workflow with Makefile
description: Create a root Makefile to handle frontend builds, backend compilation, and execution of the testserver. Ensure 'run_viewer.sh' is optimized to avoid redundant npm installs.

title: Frontend Build Cleanup & TS Configuration
description: Update frontend/tsconfig.json to prevent .js emission into src. Clean up existing compiled artifacts and ensure Vite handles transpilation correctly.

title: Automated Integration Testing with Testserver
description: Integrate cmd/testserver into the standard 'go test' workflow. Create 'Golden Path' tests in pkg/computer/integration_test.go that verify click, type, and scroll actions against the local bench.

title: Implement Structured Tool Metadata in Registry
description: Upgrade the tool registry to use a ToolDefinition struct instead of a raw function. Include metadata for Source (Standard, Skill, Patch, Utility) and Category. This enables better logging and programmatic prompt construction.

title: Implement Computer Use Main Loop
description: Refactor main.go to support the AI loop: Screenshot -> Prompt -> Action -> Repeat.

title: Implement Euclidean Aim Assist
description: Replace hierarchy-based focus fallback with a spatial distance check to find the nearest input element to the click coordinates.

title: Debug TUI formatting and log interleaving
description: Identify why logs are breaking the TUI layout. Likely cause: log.Printf calls in executor.go and computer.go are writing to stderr/stdout while Bubble Tea is managing the screen. Need to redirect log output to a file or pipe it through the Observer.

title: Implement Semantic Scrolling (Directional)
description: The scroll handler currently only supports delta_x/y and coordinates. It needs to support semantic directions like 'down', 'up', 'left', 'right' by mapping them to pixel deltas (e.g., 500px).

title: Make Viewport Dimensions Configurable
description: Add CLI flags (e.g., --width, --height) to allow users to override the default 1280x1024 viewport. Ensure these are passed correctly to Chromedp and the Executor for coordinate denormalization.

title: Support .env configuration
description: Add support for loading GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT and GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION from a .env file if present. This improves local development ergonomics. Create a sample .env.example file and update the README.

title: Enhance Typing Reliability
description: Replace chromedp.KeyEvent with chromedp.SendKeys or JS injection to ensure text entry works even if physical key simulation is flaky.

title: Programmatic CAPTCHA Detection & Intervention
description: The model doesn't always emit a 'safety_decision' when blocked. We need to: 1. Periodically check the DOM for 'Our systems have detected unusual traffic' or reCAPTCHA iframe patterns. 2. If detected, emit a specialized computer.EventSafety to trigger the TUI y/n prompt manually.

title: Distinguish Session End State in TUI
description: The TUI currently displays 'Session Finished' regardless of the reason. Update the computer package to emit distinct statuses for 'Goal Achieved' vs 'Max Turns Reached', and update the TUI to display this distinction (e.g., ✅ Finished vs 🛑 Max Turns).

title: Session Viewer: Log & Gallery View
description: Build key components: 1. <session-list>: A sidebar/grid of sessions with metadata (date, prompt). 2. <session-detail>: Scrollable log viewer + turn-by-turn screenshot gallery. 3. <screenshot-turn>: A card showing the thinking, action, and resulting image for a single turn.

title: Optimize Agent Context Window
description: Implement history management strategies to prevent token exhaustion and reduce latency in long-running sessions, inspired by the Python reference implementation's screenshot pruning.

title: Prune Old Screenshots
description: Update computer.Run loop to iterate through history and remove InlineData (images) from User turns older than N (e.g. 3) turns. Keep the function response text/metadata. Reference: agent.py MAX_RECENT_TURN_WITH_SCREENSHOTS.

title: Session Viewer: Image Lightbox with Metadata
description: Add a lightbox feature to the Session Viewer. Clicking on a screenshot (viewport or full-page) should open it in a near-fullscreen modal overlay. This modal should include a sidebar displaying metadata about the image (timestamp, step index, action taken) and allow navigation between images.

title: Save debug screenshots
description: Save screenshots to disk (screenshots/turn_X.png) for debugging.

title: Concepts & Explainers
description: Expand documentation with deep dives on Security, Skills, and Wasm integration.

title: Write Doc: Secure Automation Patterns
description: Document best practices for handling auth tokens and secrets in browser agents.

title: Auto-Click Buttons in Aim Assist
description: Update executor.go to call el.click() if the spatial aim assist finds a BUTTON or SUBMIT input, ensuring form submission works.

title: Implement Ctrl+C Handler
description: Add graceful shutdown handling to main.go to ensure resources (browser, ffmpeg) are cleaned up on interrupt.

title: Resilience & Experimentation
description: Experiments to validate agent behavior against local servers and edge cases.

title: Create Go Test Server
description: Create a simple Go HTTP server with standardized HTML elements (buttons, inputs) for controlled agent testing.

title: Define Resilience Scenarios
description: Document specific scenarios like network failure, 404s, and CAPTCHAs to test agent recovery.

title: Refine Input & Logging
description: Improve typing reliability and compact the debug logs.

title: Log Screenshot Paths
description: Update computer.go to include the saved screenshot filename in the EventLog/EventStatus payload for easier correlation.

title: Session Viewer: Investigate Real-time Updates via WebSockets
description: The current session viewer requires a refresh to show new turns. Investigate and plan the implementation of a WebSocket connection between the backend and the frontend to push updates (new logs/turns/screenshots) in real-time.

title: Refine README positioning
description: Update README to frame the project as a foundational framework for browser agents.

title: Debug 400 Invalid Argument
description: Add request/history logging to debug the API error.

title: Improve TUI Log Contrast
description: The current log color (#3C3C3C) is too dark/low-contrast on many dark terminal backgrounds. Change 'infoStyle' in pkg/tui/tui.go to a lighter gray (e.g., #AAAAAA or adaptive) to ensure readability.

title: Skill Injection System
description: Develop patterns for injecting external logic (JS/Wasm) into the browser context to extend agent capabilities.

title: Experiment: The Highlighter Skill
description: Create a 'Highlighter' skill that injects JS to colorize specific DOM elements, helping the AI see them.

title: Implement Computed Style Mapping
description: Enable the assistant to 'see' the computed CSS of a clicked element for layout analysis (e.g., margins, alignments).

title: Implement 'Full Page Scan' Skill
description: Use chromedp to extract a list of all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, sliders) with their labels and coordinates. Provide this as a text 'minimap' to the model to improve spatial awareness.

title: Augment with Full-Page Screenshots
description: Experiment with providing a downsampled full-page screenshot alongside the high-res viewport screenshot so the model 'sees' the page length and structure.

title: Document Lessons Learned
description: Create docs/lessons_learned.md capturing insights on coordinate drift, focus management, and model hallucinations.

title: Session Viewer: Lit Frontend Scaffold
description: Scaffold Lit frontend using Vite and TypeScript. 1. Use @vaadin/router for navigation. 2. Implement App Shell with Material Web components. 3. Setup API client for fetching session data.

title: Fix hardcoded URL
description: Modify executor.go to retrieve the actual URL from the browser after every action instead of returning a hardcoded placeholder.

title: Augment with Accessibility Tree
description: Instead of just screenshots, provide the model with a simplified Accessibility Tree (AXTree). This helps the model identify interactive elements and their current values without relying solely on visual interpretation.

title: Handle open_web_browser tool
description: Implement the 'open_web_browser' tool call which appears to be a predefined function.

title: Core Stability & UX
description: Polish the local CLI experience with better reporting and output formats.

title: Implement Structured Audit Mode
description: Add a --mode audit flag that returns a JSON report of 'Visual Health' (e.g., contrast violations, elements overflowing containers, or inconsistent margins).

title: Add Pre-flight Diagnostics
description: Check if a 'blank' browser page can be launched before starting the model loop, providing a clear 'Environment Not Ready' error instead of a timeout.

title: Implement handleScroll in Executor
description: Implement real scrolling logic in pkg/computer/executor.go using window.scrollBy or similar, supporting deltaX and deltaY from the model's tool calls.

title: Implement handleWait in Executor
description: Implement explicit waiting logic in pkg/computer/executor.go to allow the model to wait for page loads or animations.

title: Implement Per-Turn Timeouts
description: Add context timeouts to GenerateContent and chromedp calls to prevent silent hangs. If a turn takes more than 60s, it should error out with a clear diagnostic.

title: Implement CAPTCHA/Bot Detection Handling
description: The model often gets blocked by Google's 'unusual traffic' detector. We need to: 1. Programmatically detect these blocks (e.g., checking for specific text or URLs). 2. Implement a 'Human-in-the-loop' trigger or suggest a pivot (like navigating directly to a domain).

title: Cloud Native Infrastructure
description: Enable running the agent on Cloud Run with GCS storage for distributed automation.

title: Dockerize with Headless Chrome
description: Create a Dockerfile that packages the Go binary with a compatible headless Chrome shell for Cloud Run.

title: Abstract Storage Layer
description: Refactor file saving to an interface to support both Local Disk and GCS backends.

title: Write Architectural Primer
description: Create docs/concepts.md to codify the 'Browser OS' architecture, mapping Driver, Kernel, Skills, and Avatar concepts.

title: Implement JS Focus Fallback
description: Enhance handleMouseClick to use document.elementFromPoint(x,y).focus() to ensure input fields are activated even if physical clicks are slightly off.

title: Resilient Tool Handling (Anti-Hallucination)
description: The model sometimes hallucinates tools not in our config (e.g., 'search'). When we respond to these, the API returns a 400. We need to: 1. Map common hallucinations to existing tools. 2. Gracefully drop unknown tool responses from history to prevent API errors.

title: Implement Drag & Drop Skill
description: Extend executor.go to support complex interactions like Drag & Drop and Hover, enabling the agent to use Kanban boards, sliders, and map interfaces.

title: Implement DragAndDrop in Executor
description: Add 'drag_and_drop' to the tool definition and implement it in executor.go using chromedp.Mouse.Down/Move/Up sequence. Verify with a Kanban test case.

title: Implement Hover in Executor
description: Add 'hover_at' to the tool definition and executor.go. Useful for triggering flyout menus or tooltips before clicking.

title: Implement Chromedp Executor
description: Map Gemini FunctionCalls to Chromedp actions (click, type, etc).

title: Truncate logs
description: Truncate base64 image data in debug logs to reduce noise.

title: Refine TUI Event Stream
description: Update computer.go to emit detailed, structured events for TUI rendering instead of dumping the raw JSON history.

title: Interactive Web UI (Watch Mode)
description: Real-time web interface to watch the agent's progress via WebSocket streaming.

title: Implement WebSocket Hub
description: Create a thread-safe WebSocket hub in Go to broadcast session updates to connected frontend clients. This includes streaming screenshots, log entries, and turn updates in real-time to the Session Viewer, removing the need for manual refreshes.

title: Scaffold Watch UI
description: Create a simple HTML/JS frontend to connect to the WebSocket and display the live image stream.

title: Improve Interaction Reliability (Click/Drag Debugging)
description: Interactions often miss or trigger unwanted behaviors like auto-scrolling. We need to: 1. Log the element under the click coordinates. 2. Verify viewport vs window sizing during interaction. 3. Add more granular control over JS focus assist.

title: Testing Infrastructure & Verification
description: Core testing and verification tools including unit tests and visual logging.

title: Unit Tests for Executor
description: Write Go unit tests for pkg/computer/executor.go to verify GenAI tool mapping.

title: Visual Verification GIF
description: Add a --gif flag to main.go to compile screenshots into a GIF using ffmpeg.

title: Secure Avatar & Auth
description: Implement secure session persistence and secret injection to allow authenticated agent workflows.

title: Implement Cookie Injection
description: Add a flag --cookies-file to main.go that loads JSON cookies and injects them into the chromedp context.

title: Agent Observability & Debugging
description: Advanced debugging tools including 'Second Pair of Eyes' analysis and automated verification of agent actions.

title: Experiment: Second Pair of Eyes
description: Create a script that uses a lightweight model (Gemini Flash) to audit the 'post' screenshots and verify if the intended action had a visual effect.

title: Integrate Test Server into Dev Workflow
description: The cmd/testserver should be officially supported as a way to verify agent skills (scrolling, waiting, etc.) without external dependencies.

description: Investigate and resolve 'cannot unmarshal JSON string into Go network.CookiePartitionKey' and 'unknown IPAddressSpace value' errors. These are likely caused by mismatches between the browser version and cdproto.

Allow users to define their own mappings via config. 3. Implement a 'Reflection' fallback where Gemini is used to rewrite the model's failed intent into a valid tool call.

description: Add CLI flags (e.g., --width, --height) to allow users to override the default 1280x1024 viewport. Ensure these are passed correctly to Chromedp and the Executor for coordinate denormalization.
